_meta.purpose: Discovery-mode engagement questionnaire — first taxonomy draft for review BEFORE any UI work. See project_engagement_questionnaire_2026_05_02.md for full design rationale.
_meta.status: draft for review — not wired into the app
_meta.shape: Flat node lookup table; rootId points to entry node; question nodes have options that point to next nodes; leaf nodes hold suggestions.
_meta.constraints: ≤10 questions in any path. Each leaf has 3-15 items. Suggestions are existing program ids (programs.js) or entry ids (legal/entries/*.js). Discovery framing only — never assert eligibility, always 'might qualify, call to confirm'.
rules: Sensitive signals (DV, immigration, suicide) route through SENSITIVE-set gating with content warnings — never auto-suggest by surprise.
Each suggestion carries a brief 'why' string explaining the link to the user's answers.
Adaptive: a 'no kids' answer in a parent branch should skip child-related sub-branches rather than ask irrelevant questions.
_meta.productBar: Per Anthony 2026-05-02: this is the true product. Every linked entry/program must be attorney- or professional-verified before the questionnaire ships under HelpFinder's banner. Current state: most items carry factCheckedBy (web-search) or verified (program), NOT attorney review. Anchors are the only attorney-tier review path; 6 anchors live as of 2026-05-02 and even those are provisional pending sign-off.
_meta.reviewPolicy: Two-track ship threshold (decided 2026-05-02 afternoon): entries ship at anchor or attorney-reviewed; programs ship at verified-program (fc stamp from web-search drain). Programs surface factual contact info — not legal-interpretation — so professional verification is the right bar; conflating program metadata with attorney review dilutes both terms.

start.text: What's the biggest thing on your mind right now?
start.subtext: Pick the one that fits best — you can come back and try other paths.

options.label: Money is tight or bills are piling up
Health, doctor, or medication problem
Something at work or losing my job
Where I live — eviction, repairs, no place to go
Family — kids, breakup, caregiving, baby on the way
Something about my situation — record, immigration, age, disability
Something else / not sure

money-which.text: Which of these is the biggest pain right now?

options.label: Can't pay rent or behind on rent
Not enough food in the house
Behind on heat / electric / gas
Drowning in debt or credit-card calls
Need cash now and don't know where to start

leaf-money-rent.summary: Behind on rent has more options than most people realize. Here's a starting list — none of these require a lawyer.

items.why: Emergency rent / one-shot deal at Monroe County DSS — apply in person at 691 St Paul
Rent assistance + case management; specific zips
What the landlord has to do BEFORE eviction — buys time and may surface defenses
Rent-withholding may be available if the unit has serious problems the landlord won't fix
Call 211 — they coordinate emergency rental assistance and can route to current programs

leaf-money-food.summary: Several things stack on top of each other — most don't require an income check.

items.why: Monthly food benefit on an EBT card
What rights SNAP recipients have — recertification, fair hearings, post-conviction eligibility
Free pantry — no paperwork, no income check at most locations
If pregnant, postpartum, or kids under 5 — extra free food + nutrition counseling
If RCSD student — free breakfast and lunch automatically
Rochester Public Market — Double Up matches SNAP $1-for-$1 on produce
Meals on Wheels if 60+ or homebound

leaf-money-utilities.summary: Utility shutoff has a specific path — and there's a separate program for the equipment itself.

items.why: Help with heating bills; emergency HEAP if heat is shut off
If 60+ and own the home — pays up to $4-8K to repair or replace the furnace
RG&E payment plans — call BEFORE shutoff
Free home insulation, air sealing, heat pumps — lowers future bills

leaf-money-debt.summary: Free help here — be wary of for-profit 'debt relief' companies.

items.why: Rochester Financial Empowerment Center — free 1-on-1 debt counseling
JustCause monthly bankruptcy clinic — find out if Chapter 7 or 13 fits
Free coaching and financial counseling

leaf-money-cash.summary: There's no single 'just give me money' program, but a few benefits add up. The state's myBenefits portal is the one application that covers most.

items.why: One application — SNAP + Medicaid + cash assistance + HEAP + childcare in one
Temporary Assistance — monthly cash for rent, utilities, basics
Earned Income Tax Credit — refundable, you may have unclaimed past years
CASH/VITA does free tax prep — can help recover past EITC
How to prove SNAP/Medicaid/cash receipt to landlord, employer, court — uniform letter from each agency

targetQueries: what benefits do i qualify for

health-which.text: What's the most pressing health issue?

options.label: No insurance / lost insurance
Can't afford prescriptions
Mental health crisis or addiction
Need a doctor / dentist and can't pay

leaf-health-insurance.summary: Most NY adults under 65 apply through NY State of Health. Different doors for different situations.

items.why: Free or very low cost — covers doctors, hospital, dental, mental health, prescriptions
If kids under 19 — free or low-cost coverage with a per-income premium
One application covers Medicaid + cash + SNAP + HEAP

targetQueries: no insurance what to do ny

leaf-health-rx.summary: Three things stack here — and the state has a senior-specific program most people miss.

items.why: Free discount card — up to 80% off, no application, use at CVS or Wegmans
EPIC — NY's prescription program for seniors 65+ on Medicare
Trillium pharmacy — sliding scale, no insurance required

leaf-health-crisis.summary: If in immediate danger to self or others, call 988 right now or go to a Strong walk-in. Otherwise these are the next-best paths.
leaf-health-crisis.warning: If suicide or immediate self-harm: call or text 988 — free, 24/7. Spanish text: AYUDA to 988.

items.why: Suicide and crisis lifeline — 24/7, includes addiction crisis
Strong Memorial Psych Emergency — walk-in 24/7, no appointment
BHACC — walk-in crisis center, no appointment, daily 9am-9pm
Trillium Health behavioral health — sliding scale

leaf-health-clinic.summary: Sliding-scale clinics see anyone who walks in — Jordan and Trillium take everyone insured or not.

items.why: Jordan Health Center — primary care, dental, mental health, walk-ins welcome
Trillium Health — primary care, sliding scale, LGBTQ+ healthcare leader
Eastman Dental — sliding scale dental, walk-ins for emergencies
Planned Parenthood — reproductive health + birth control + STI testing

options.label: Got fired or laid off
Hurt at work
Boss isn't paying me / wage theft
Looking for work / training

leaf-work-fired.summary: Some firings are unlawful even in an at-will state. Filing has hard deadlines.

items.why: When a firing is against the law — discrimination, retaliation, whistleblower
Temporary Assistance to bridge until next paycheck
Apply right away — SNAP processes faster after job loss
Lost-job often qualifies for Medicaid even if it didn't before

leaf-work-injury.summary: Workers' comp covers job injuries — including from desk-job repetitive strain. Don't miss the filing window.

items.why: How NY workers' comp works — what's covered, deadlines, retaliation protection
LawNY — free legal help for low-income workers including comp denials
Worker Justice Center — wage theft + retaliation + injury cases

leaf-work-wages.summary: Wage theft is illegal regardless of immigration status. NY has strong remedies.

items.why: What counts as wage theft + how to file with NY DOL
Worker Justice Center of NY — wage theft, retaliation, harassment cases for low-wage workers; represents regardless of immigration status
LawNY — free representation in employment cases for low-income workers

leaf-work-training.summary: Multiple paths — RochesterWorks for adults, Learn2Earn for older teens.

items.why: Learn2Earn — paid internships and career exploration for older teens
Rochester Financial Empowerment Center — free coaching including career and budget planning

options.label: Got an eviction notice or court papers
No place to sleep / homeless
Apartment is unsafe — heat, mold, leaks, no lock
Landlord won't return my security deposit

leaf-housing-eviction.summary: Eviction has a process. Court papers do NOT mean you have to move that day.

items.why: What the landlord has to do BEFORE you can be removed — the timeline + cure window
Emergency one-shot rent through Monroe DSS, in-person at 691 St Paul
JustCause free legal clinics — online intake at legalhelprochester.com
LawNY — free housing legal help across 14 counties

leaf-housing-homeless.summary: Walk-in shelter exists tonight. Daytime services (food + clothing + case management) stack on top.

items.why: House of Mercy — emergency overnight shelter open 24/7, no ID required
Community Place — case management + food + clothing + advocacy
Temporary Assistance can fund placement into transitional housing

leaf-housing-unsafe.summary: NY has the warranty of habitability — every NY landlord makes this promise automatically. Lease can't waive it.

items.why: What the warranty of habitability actually covers + tenant remedies
Lead-paint hazards in pre-1978 rentals — RRP rules, landlord disclosure, what to do
JustCause clinics — landlord-tenant cases
LawNY — direct representation in unsafe-conditions cases

leaf-housing-deposit.summary: NY caps deposits at 1 month and gives a 14-day return deadline. Missing the deadline forfeits the right to keep ANY of it.

items.why: Anchor on GOL § 7-108 — 14-day rule, walk-throughs, what the landlord can and can't deduct
JustCause Legal Clinics — free landlord-tenant clinic, online intake at legalhelprochester.com
LawNY — free housing legal help across 14 counties including security-deposit cases

targetQueries: one month rule security deposit ny

options.label: Pregnant or new baby
Divorce, separation, custody
Caring for an older parent or disabled adult

leaf-family-baby.summary: Several stacking benefits — and NY's new Paid Prenatal Leave is unique.

items.why: WIC — free healthy food + nutrition counseling for pregnancy + kids under 5
Pregnancy Medicaid available even at higher incomes; kids under 1 covered automatically
Child Health Plus — kids under 19, scales with income

leaf-family-split.summary: Free family-law clinics exist — and child support intake is its own line at DSS.

items.why: How NY calculates child support + how to modify orders
JustCause Family Law Clinic — custody + orders of protection

leaf-family-split.warning: If the situation involves abuse or fear of harm — yours, your kids', or anyone in your house — call NY State DV Hotline 1-800-942-6906 (24/7, confidential, multilingual) or text 844-997-2121. The free family-law options below are for separation, custody, and child support; for safety planning, the hotline is the right first call.

leaf-family-caregiving.summary: EISEP is the in-home help program most people miss. Office for the Aging is the front door for seniors.

items.why: Office for the Aging — meals, transportation, Medicare help, caregiver support
Free in-home help for seniors 60+ NOT on Medicaid — personal care + housekeeping
Meals on Wheels — hot meals delivered if 60+ or homebound

targetQueries: in home care for senior ny

options.label: I have a criminal record from years ago
Just turned 60 or older
Have a disability
Recently released from incarceration

leaf-status-record.summary: NY's Clean Slate Act now seals most older convictions automatically — most people don't realize they qualify.

items.why: Anchor — automatic sealing of older convictions; nothing to file
Worker Justice Center — represents people with records facing employment discrimination
SNAP eligibility after a conviction — most felony bars eliminated under recent NY changes
Call 211 — connects to current reentry programs in Monroe County

leaf-status-senior.summary: Office for the Aging is the front door. EPIC for prescriptions is uniquely NY.

items.why: Office for the Aging — meals + transport + Medicare help + caregiver support
NY's prescription program for seniors on Medicare
Meals on Wheels if homebound
Free sit-down lunch at 20 senior centers across Monroe

leaf-status-disability.summary: ABLE accounts let people with disabilities save without losing SSI / Medicaid. Most people don't know about them.

items.why: How NY ABLE accounts work — tax-advantaged savings without losing SSI/Medicaid
Center for Disability Rights — advocacy, ABLE account help, benefits planning; run by people with disabilities

leaf-status-reentry.summary: Reentry has multiple stacking supports. SNAP and Medicaid often re-activate post-release.

items.why: Most older convictions seal automatically under Clean Slate Act
SNAP after conviction — most felony bars eliminated under recent NY changes

leaf-not-sure.summary: When the situation is hard to name, two paths cover the most ground: 211 connects to anything, and HelpFinder's main questionnaire walks through specifics.

items.why: Call 211 (or 1-877-356-9211) — staffed routing for any situation, 24/7
myBenefits.ny.gov — one application covers SNAP + Medicaid + HEAP + cash + childcare

targetQueries: do i qualify for help ny
